channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg once gave a Facebook engineer startup advice at 2 a.m. while ‘hanging out with all the interns’—she quit and raised millions after
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg told his former Facebook engineer Sophie Novati: "Figure out a way to capture people's valuable attention."

item.title: The ‘PayPal Mafia’ built a $1.5 billion fintech pioneer. The company they left behind is on life support
item.description: Fortune coined the term "PayPal Mafia" in 2007. Two decades later, the mafia is thriving, but PayPal isn't.

item.title: It’s always happy hour at the airport bar, but Ryanair’s CEO is calling for a crackdown on 6am tipples: ‘Who needs to be drinking beer at that time?’
item.description: A familiar vice is threatening the raw liberty of being in an airport.

item.title: Trump’s AI policy team came into office opposing everything Biden did. Now it’s on the cusp of implementing many of the same policies
item.description: After railing against Biden-era AI safety efforts, the Trump administration is now weighing its own frontier-model oversight as fears grow around cybe...
